title: RDP session = full screen is not full screen
author: wellloaded
body_snippet:
Sorry for the twisted title but this is essentially what I am trying to say:

There are 2x full screen mode in mRemoteNG

-F11 = no problem and straight forward

-right click on open rdp session tab and select full screen

This latter is in my opinion not behaving as expected. Essentially it goes in full screen but without reconnecting (hence renegotiating the maximum resolution). I would have expected the Full screen toggle for rdp to always trigger an rdp re-connection. Unless there's ...

iterations.description: The RDP client's `FullScreen` property should automatically handle resolution when entering full screen. Explicitly setting `DesktopWidth` and `DesktopHeight` when `FullScreen` is true might cause conflicts or incorrect scaling, leading to the reported issue.

comments.author: LIyoai
comments.snippet: Switching to full screen leaves black borders #1402

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**
**Branch:** `main`
**What changed:** Remove the explicit assignment of `_rdpClient.DesktopWidth` 

**Download latest beta:** **[CENSORED!] Links to external GitHub repositories are not allowed**

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---
_Automated by mRemoteNG Issue Intelligence System_

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The RDP client's `FullScreen` property should automatically handle resolution when entering full screen. Explicitly setting `DesktopWidth` and `DesktopHeight` when `FullScreen` is true might cause conflicts or incorrect scaling, leading to the reported issue.
Approach: Remove the explicit assignment of `_rdpClient.DesktopWidth` and `_rdpClient.DesktopHeight` within the `SetResolution()` method of `RdpProtocol.cs` when `_rdpClient.FullScreen` is set to true.
